name: Scales
epithet: The Assassin Who Changed His Mind
bio: Scales is a French-born global assassin of the highest order, which means there are very few contracts he would decline and several he has declined anyway, because one of the things that makes him exceptional is that he does his own research before accepting an assignment and trusts his assessment over a client's briefing. His assignment in Valmere gave him pause before he accepted it. The reconnaissance was low-risk. The secondary objective — eliminate a High Council member if the opportunity arose — seemed unlikely enough to be theoretical.

He infiltrated Valmere using methods that were designed specifically to stay below Clive's detection range, which he had studied carefully before attempting, because he had read about the telepath in the intelligence community's back-channel conversations about Valmere and had decided that knowing the range was worth the research time. He spent three days inside. He did his reconnaissance. He found a country that functioned the way it said it did.

He left without attempting the secondary objective. He has not discussed this with his client, or with anyone. He brought accurate intelligence back to whoever hired him, and Valmere chose not to pursue him because they had no objection to accurate information about Valmere reaching the world. Whether he will take another contract against Valmere depends on who asks, for what, and what his research tells him this time. His hesitation has not resolved into principle. It has resolved into a specific reluctance that he keeps as private as everything else.
